meta_title: For Freelancers: design with AI to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to design with AI and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Design with AI to Solve Debugging Woes
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with broken code. Learn how to design with AI using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship faster, impress clients, and debug like a pro.
content.answer_block: When you're stuck in a debugging loop, don't waste hours chasing syntax errors. Shift your workflow: use Antigravity to architect your component logic visually, then leverage Claude Code to implement and verify the code against your design. This AI-first approach turns complex debugging into a streamlined, automated validation process.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a screen at 2 AM. The client’s deadline is in twelve hours, the production build is throwing an obscure runtime error, and the stack trace looks like a cryptic alien language. We’ve all been there—the 'debugging wall' is the fastest way for a freelancer to burn out and lose profit.

But the era of manual, line-by-line debugging is ending. By moving your focus from fixing broken text to designing functional logic with AI, you transform your role from a code janitor to an architect. This guide shows you how to leverage modern AI tools to bypass the syntax grind and deliver rock-solid applications that actually work the first time.

steps.title: Architecting Logic in Antigravity
steps.description: Before writing a single line of code, map out your state transitions and logic flow using Antigravity to ensure the architecture is sound.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a high-level visual architecture for a user authentication flow including error states and token refresh logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude read ./architecture.json --task 'Generate boilerplate structure based on this flow'

steps.title: Context-Aware Implementation
steps.description: Feed your architectural design directly into Claude Code to generate implementation files that follow your specific constraints.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate the interface definitions and state management boilerplate based on the visual graph.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write --context ./architecture.json --prompt 'Implement the defined state machine in TypeScript'

steps.title: Automated Error Isolation
steps.description: When a bug occurs, use Claude Code to scan your logs and compare the execution path against your original design.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the log pattern against the intended state flow to find the logical deviation.

steps.title: Refactoring via AI Design
steps.description: Instead of patching code, update the design in Antigravity and let the AI regenerate the surrounding unit tests.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the data flow to handle edge-case null values identified during the last test run.
steps.claude_code_command: claude update --refactor --test-coverage 'Ensure all new edges in the design are covered by tests'

steps.title: Final Verification & Deploy
steps.description: Perform a final automated audit of your code against your design constraints to ensure zero regressions before client delivery.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a final audit checklist comparing the codebase against the visual architecture graph.

pro_tips: Always treat your Antigravity graph as the 'Source of Truth'—if the code differs from the graph, update the graph first to keep the AI aligned.
Use Claude Code's '--diff' flag to review AI suggestions before applying them; it prevents the AI from accidentally overwriting your custom CSS or specific business logic.
When debugging, provide the AI with the specific 'before' and 'after' states from your design to help it pinpoint where the state transition failed.
Chain multiple prompts by saving intermediate outputs to a 'context.md' file; this keeps the AI from losing track of your project requirements during long sessions.
Treat your AI interactions like a pairing session: ask the AI 'Why did you choose this implementation pattern?' to learn and improve your own coding intuition.
resources: Effective Prompt Engineering for Devs: https://anthropic.com/prompt-guide
content.next_steps: After completing this workflow, implement a small side project using this exact cycle to build muscle memory before applying it to a high-stakes client project.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your code and start architecting your success. By automating the grunt work, you gain more time to focus on high-value client features that actually scale. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.

Feed your architectural design directly into Claude Code to generate implementation files that follow your specific constraints.
When a bug occurs, use Claude Code to scan your logs and compare the execution path against your original design.
Instead of patching code, update the design in Antigravity and let the AI regenerate the surrounding unit tests.
Perform a final automated audit of your code against your design constraints to ensure zero regressions before client delivery.
